refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policies and safety plans
Produce an abstracted spatial representation focusing on key features required for planning and execution
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policies and safety plans

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with safety and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy layers, the design supports independence and easier updates without cross-impact.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data and up-to-date operational policies
Risk that combining multiple data types in one model may increase complexity and maintenance effort
Potential challenge in ensuring model updates propagate correctly to planning/execution functions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the modular boundaries within the worksite model to separate terrain representation from policy and safety annotations to maintain independence and ease of updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: This module encompasses the integration and processing of terrain data, operational policies, and safety plans to develop a modular worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and operational data inputs.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts based on safety plan evaluations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other system modules for data exchange and coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is designed for integrating complex data and policies; minimal diffusion expected.
Vehicle module focuses on physical control; containing the worksite model here would cause high diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation handles remote control; worksite model inclusion would diffuse logic and data handling.
Safety Services relates to safety plans but lacks scope for terrain and operational policy integration, causing moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the DP as it is designed to integrate diverse data sources and policies into a cohesive worksite model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services on safety plan aspects may cause interface complexity.
Dependence on accurate and timely telemetry data may affect module performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying the development of a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policies and safety plans, directly fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services on safety plan aspects may cause interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary for the DP, showing high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. The module effectively integrates terrain data, operational policies, and safety plans, minimizing diffusion compared to other modules with lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module's outputs (worksite model/map) can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability to planning/execution functions and inclusion of agreed site features, safety plans, and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with explicit scope and interfaces defined, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize the interface and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Safety Services to manage overlap on safety plan aspects and reduce interface complexity.
Establish monitoring and fallback mechanisms to handle dependencies on telemetry data to mitigate performance risks.
Document acceptance test cases explicitly linking the worksite model outputs to planning and execution functions to strengthen verification.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition is sufficient. The DP logically addresses the FR, the IM boundary is well chosen with high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are recommended revisions to clarify interfaces and document test cases, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level and focus on implementing the recommended revisions to manage interface complexity and verification rigor.

revise.why: To clarify and formalize the interface and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Safety Services to manage overlap on safety plan aspects and reduce interface complexity, and to document acceptance test cases explicitly linking the worksite model outputs to planning and execution functions to strengthen verification.

assumptions: The current DP fully captures the functional requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for the DP.
Verification methods and acceptance criteria are sufficient to validate the worksite model outputs.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to overlap with Safety Services on safety plan aspects.
Dependency on telemetry data may pose performance risks if not properly monitored.
evidence_needed: Formalized interface and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Documented acceptance test cases linking worksite model outputs to planning and execution functions.
Monitoring and fallback mechanisms for telemetry data dependencies.
